texts.title: Title 1
texts.original:
Ole terve, neitsikene
Ole terve kasvamasta
Oma au hoidemasta
Nüid sa saad saele sõita.
Isameestel hüpitada

Oleks läind metsast mehele
Puu otsast poistelegi
Kännu otsast kaasadelle.

Puud oleks olnud pulmanaised
Kased valged kaasanaised
Sööditükk su söömalauda
Kanarbik su hoostekarja
Põllupeenar istepinki.

Nüid sind kaasale kositi.
Nüid on sõitnud saekene
Piitsaplaksud, peniplaksud

Sõitis äia õue alla
Sõitis ämma õue alla
Tule ämma, too kasukast
Tule vahest vaatamaie
Vii see tige teelta
Vii see rine reelta
Too kui tonti tubaje

Ämma silma, ämma kõrva
Pilusilma, peni kõrva
Kõverkäppa ämma ette

Liigu nüid liigu, leekene
Anna ruumi ahjukene
Tagane tagumine seina
Nüid tuleb trummal tubaje Parapantsik põrmandulle,

Kui ma tuiskan ja vihastan
Võtan kulbi koera eesta
Leemevaagna litsi käästa
Annan kulbi eide kätte
Leemekausi eide rüppe.
texts.english:
Be healthy, little maiden
Be healthy while growing
Upkeep your honor
Now you may ride the saw.
For the men to jump

It would have gone from the forest to the man
From the tree to the boys
From the stump to the companions.

The trees would have been the brides
The white birches the bridesmaids
The feeding piece your eating board
Heather your feeding flock
The garden patch a bench.

Now you are courted as a companion.
Now the little saw has ridden
Whip taps, whip slaps

Rode under the father-in-law’s yard
Rode under the mother-in-law’s yard
Come, mother-in-law, bring the fur coat
Come maybe to watch
Take that anger from the road
Take the grudge from the path
Bring it to the devil’s room

The mother-in-law’s eye, mother-in-law’s ear
The slit eye, the dog’s ear
The crooked foot before the mother-in-law

Move now, little flame
Make way, little stove
Step back to the back wall
Now here comes the drumming room Parapantsik to the ground
When I whirl and get angry
I take the ladle from the dog
I turn the soup dish upside down
I give the ladle to the old woman
I give the soup bowl to the old woman to drink.

texts.title: Kiusulaul pulmas.
texts.original:
Oh sina sigade saia,
Kõige pere pesukappa,
Isa, ema kätekappa,
Alga aga üles annivakka,
Kisu aga üles kiidukerstu,

Mis sina mullu kiitelesid
Meie küla kiige alla,
Meie valla vainuella:
"Oleks mul sada naduda,
Oleks mul kümme küdida!"

Naisukesed, kaasukesed,
Las siis küsin kannatelles.
Kas siis äia annab härja?
Kas siis ämma annab lehma?
Kas siis küdi annab küüdu?
Nadu valgepea vasika?

Küll siis äia saaneb särgi,
Küll siis ämma saaneb särgi,
Küdi soab küüdud searepaelad,
Nadu narmikud käiksed.

Tasa aga neiut tantsitage
Viisipärast veeretage,
Korrapärast keerutage,
Et ei põru põllepaelad,
Ega ei liigu mütsilindid.
Ehk on tited tiiva alla,
Värdjad vöö vahella,
Kasulapsed kaendelassa.
texts.english:
Oh you pig’s bread,
The family’s laundry cupboard,
Father, mother’s hand cupboard,
Start then to give the gift,
Pull then up the praise basket,

What did you boast about last year
Under our village swing,
Under our parish guardian:
“If only I had a hundred calves,
If only I had ten cows!”

Women, companions,
Then let me ask patiently.
Does the father-in-law give a bull?
Does the mother-in-law give a cow?
Does the cowherd give a cow?
The white-headed calf of the herd?

Surely then the father-in-law will get a shirt,
Surely then the mother-in-law will get a shirt,
The cowherd will have the ribbon of a cowherd’s belt,
The herd boy will have her shoes.

Softly then, girls, dance
Carefully roll to the tune,
Spin in order,
So the apron ribbons do not snap,
Nor do the hatbands move.
Maybe the children under the wing,
The sacks between the belt,
The foster children in the cradle.

texts.title: Title 3
texts.original:
Peiukene, poisikene
Kust oskasid meile tulla?
Oskasid üle oraste
Märkasid üle mägede
Teil olid tulgid tullessagi
Keelekoerad kaasassagi.
texts.english:
Little boy, little lad
Where did you know to come to us from?
You knew to come over the ridges
You noticed over the mountains
You even had torches with you
And tongue dogs as well.

texts.title: Title 4
texts.original:
Kas need annid, mis ma saanud
Või on kinki, mis kingiti
Siga oli särsanud sinista
Hobu oli pursanud punasta

Kas pean pistma pingi alla
Või pean laskma laua alla
Või viin seasöötijalle
Kannan koju karjutsele.
texts.english:
Are these gifts that I have received,
or are they presents that were given?
The pig had squealed blue;
the horse had burst red.

Should I put it under the bench,
or should I let it go under the table,
or take it to the pig feeder?
I carry it home for the shouting.

texts.title: Pulmalugu pruudile
texts.original:
Õekene, ellakene,
Tuo tänna näita välja?
Mis sa kukeni kudusid
Lauba õhtuni ladusid,
Pühapäised pikad päevad,
Argipäised söömaajad.

Kuo sa peerguda põletasid,
Külimitu küünla'aida.
Kui põle anda andisida,
Anna piuga villusida.
Külap ma teel teeksin,
Suanikol´lul koorutaksin.
texts.english:
Little sister, little darling,
why do you look like that?
Why were you knitting
all day until Saturday evening,
and working on Sundays,
the long days, the everyday meal times?

Why did you burn the back of your hand,
the candle shed of the village?
If you could give a burn,
give me blisters with it.
On the way to the village,
I would peel your skin off.

texts.title: Title 6
texts.original:
Neitsikene noorukene
Kisu nüid üles kiidukerstu
Alga aidast annivakka
Mis oled eala teinud
Kasu kalli kokku pannud.
Mis sa mullu kiitelesid.
Kiitsid meie kiige alla
Hooplesid oma kodugi.

Oleks mul kümme küdida
Oleks mul sada naduda
Oleks mul viisi vennanaista
Kõik mina kataks kangastelle
Peidaks pikaje linale.
Õbelõnga õmbeleksin
Kooksin kuldakroonidesse.

Ootage urme undid
Kannatage karjakoerad
Küll te saate saamasandid
Saate õuesed agijad
Kerst mul täide kiilitud
Kõik mul täide vaalitud

Küll siin soab siis äia särgi
Äia särgi laia loadi
Ämma särki pika pihta
Nadu saab käiksed naastulised.
Küdi kindad küisilised
Kui siin äia annab ärja
Ja kui ämma kingib lehma,
Küdi siin kingib küidu härja

Küll siin anni tahtijaida
Laia vöö lunastajaid,
Ei ole ärja andijaida
Ega lehma kinkijaida.
texts.english:
A young maiden, a little cat,
now praise Kersti.
From the barn to the granary,
What have you done in your youth?
Have you gathered the precious harvest?
What did you brag about last year?
You boasted under our swing,
You showed off your home too.

If I had ten dresses,
If I had a hundred calves,
If I had five brothers-in-law,
I would cover them all with textiles,
Hide them in long linen cloth.
I would sew fine thread,
Spin into golden crowns.

Wait, wolves, wait,
Endure, shepherd dogs,
You will surely get your shoes,
You will get the outdoor coat.
Kersti, I have tightened everything,
I have woven everything full,

Here will be the father-in-law's shirt,
The father-in-law's shirt with a wide collar,
The mother-in-law's shirt with a long hem,
The calves will receive hoof bands.
Warm mittens on the fingers,
If the father-in-law gives a calf,
And if the mother-in-law gives a cow,
Warm here a young bull will be given.

Here are those who want gifts,
Wide belt redeemers,
There are no calf givers,
Nor cow donors.

texts.title: Title 1
texts.original:
Viidi meilt üks vilgas neiu
Toodi meil üks torsuperse
Naesed kiitsid põllepõlve
Austasid tanualusta
Ma olin usin uskumaie
Ja olin kaval katsumaie
Sest ep' põld põllel parema
Tanu all saab haige olla
Mütsi all saab mütsakaid
texts.english:
A lively girl was taken from us
A torsoperse was brought to us
Women praised the apron knee
They respected the threshing floor base
I was diligent in believing
And I was clever in testing
Because it’s not better to have a field on the apron
Under the threshing floor, one can get sick
Under the hat, one can get headaches

texts.title: Title 8
texts.original:
Imetasid poegalasta
Imetasid ellitasid:
Kasvatasid kallistasid
Et tooks eidele esiko
Haudujale asteliko
Tuli aega jõudis pääva
Poeg tõi eidele emanda haudujale augutaja
texts.english:
You nursed a boy child
You nursed, you sustained:
You raised, you hugged
To bring the firstborn to the mother
To the grave-digger’s doorstep
The time came, the day arrived
The son brought the mistress to the mother, the grave-digger to the pit-digger

texts.title: Title 9
texts.original:
Oiu voiu vorsti tahaks
Vorstikambre võti kadund
Mull' aga anti alvad annid
Alvad annid - hallid sukad
Kure kustud säärepaelad
Ma põld päris pulmaline.
texts.english:
Oh dear, oh dear, I would like some sausage
The key to the sausage chamber is lost
But I was given "alvad annid"
"Alvad annid" – grey stockings
Cranes' broken garters
I am quite a bride in the field.

texts.title: Title 10
texts.original:
Neitsikene noorukene
Kirjupihta peenikene
Ära sa pane pahaksi
Ära sa võta vihaksi

Et on küdi küüruselga
Et on nadu narmaskaela
Et on äia pardalõuga
Et on ämma kiukalõuga

Sest põle viga sest pole vaeva
Et on küdi küiruselga
Kodjada talo kivida

Sest põle viga sest põle vaeva
Et on nado narmaskaela
Luuata pühib tubaje

Sest põle viga sest põle vaeva
Et on äiä arvaparda
Hargita ta kütab ahjo

Sest pole viga sest pole vaeva
Et on ämma kiukalõuga
Kulbita ta keedab leeme
texts.english:
Young maiden,
slender in stature
Do not take offense
Do not get angry

Because there are folks with hunched backs
Because there are people with ragged collars
Because there are fathers-in-law with duck jaws
Because there are mothers-in-law with fretful jaws

For it is no fault, for it is no trouble
Because there are folks with hunched backs
The crooked house with stones

For it is no fault, for it is no trouble
Because there are people with ragged collars
She sweeps the tub without a bone

For it is no fault, for it is no trouble
Because there are fathers-in-law with duck jaws
Unravel it, she heats the stove

For it is no fault, for it is no trouble
Because there are mothers-in-law with fretful jaws
She stirs and cooks the broth

texts.title: Title 11
texts.original:
Oh te sandid saelised
Pulmalised peiulised
Mis te siina seisa'ate

Juba see ammu siita läinud
Paides ta pea sugenud
Palus kingad paelutanud
Peterburis teinud pulmad
Tallinas pandud tanule

Küll siin pestud peiu peeker
Pestud peiupoisi
Ihutud isame' toopi
Küll saab näia
Kas siin pestud peiu peeker
Ihutud isame' mõeka

Neitsikene noorukene
Mis sa kuulad kammerissa
Seisad seinade
Oled väetide vahele

Tule tuppa tundemaie
Võsa vasta võtemaie
Sugu suurt teretamaie
Võta võera'ad omaksi
Jäta omad võera'aksi

Neitsikene noorukene
Ei ma tule sööma tõttu
Ega joose jooma tõttu
Ma tulin lugude tõttu
Ma tulin ilu tegema

Naistel nal´la
Ma tulin saama saanitäita
Koguma tulen kolju
Oma venna õlmatäita

Et las ütlen ümmer jälle
Las laulan tagasi jälle

Ellakene, vellakene
Ära sina nea
Vannu vaga ingesida
Nea sa neidiste isada,
Nea sa neidiste emada
Vannu vana naisisida
Kis tegi neiud kõrge'eksi
Kõrge'eksi, kallieksi

Enne sain neiu nel´lakümmend
Kaasa sain kaheksadkümmend
Pulmi poole tuhandella
Nüid ep sa neiud nel´lalkümmnel
Kaasad ei kaheksalsaal
Pulmi poole tuhandella.
texts.english:
Oh, you brides, sawyers,
wedding guests, bridegrooms,
what are you standing here for?

That was long ago gone from here,
the head was shaved in Paide,
shoes tied in Palu,
weddings held in Petersburg,
the bride was given in Tallinn.

Surely here the bridegroom's face has been washed,
the bridegroom has been washed,
the young man dressed up in a suit.
We'll see
if here the bridegroom's face has been washed,
the young man dressed up in a suit.

Young maiden, young girl,
what are you listening to in the chamber?
Standing by the walls,
you are among the barren ones.

Come inside for a greeting,
take a greeting for the woods,
a big greeting for kin.
Take strangers as your own,
leave your own as strangers.

Young maiden, young girl,
I didn't come to eat
or to drink wine,
I came for stories,
I came to make beauty,

women's fun.
I came to get a wish fulfilled,
I come to gather the skull
to fill my brother's shoulder.

Let me say again,
let me sing again:

Dear one, dear one,
don't curse,
don't swear lightly,
don't curse the maiden's father,
don't curse the maiden's mother,
don't curse the old woman
who made the girls tall,
tall and dear.

Before, I had forty maidens,
I had eighty partners,
weddings numbered half a thousand.
Now you maidens are not forty,
you have no eighty partners,
weddings numbered half a thousand.

texts.title: Pulmalaul
texts.original:
Tasa neidu tansitage,
Viisi pärast veeretage!
Ehk on tited tiiva alla,
Äbarikud alla õlma.

Mini kuulis vasta kostis:
On mul äia või mul ämma!

Äi kui halli tömpi koera,
Ämm kui kõrvend arakas,
Nadu nassi kirju kassi,
Küid küirakad mõlemad,
Minu kaasa kallikene.

Ämm see tehku äbarikku
Nadu kaksi kaksikuida,
Ei mini mida kedagi!
texts.english:
Dance gently, maiden,
Roll the tune!
Maybe the little ones take wing,
The awkward ones drop their shoulders.

I heard a reply in the laughter:
Is it my father-in-law or my mother-in-law!

Father-in-law like a gray dull dog,
Mother-in-law like a scorched miser,
Filth like a spotted cat,
Both are like walking hunchbacks,
My dear spouse.

Let the mother-in-law make
the awkward one Filth like two twins,
I don’t care about anyone!

texts.title: Title 13
texts.original:
Kui sina viitsid olla virka
Ja sina tahad olla tarka
Ole hommiku usina

Enne valge'ed varane
Enne koitu kuulusama

Pühi tuba, laksi lauda
Ari akende alune
Keera kelderiesine
Pese aga peekerid pinula
Toobid toa nurga peale

Kapad köögikatuksele
Ämmerid kaevumäele
Homme tuleb kubjas külasse
Vana härra vainijulle
texts.english:
If you are willing to be alert
And you want to be wise
Be diligent in the morning

Early before dawn
Before the more famous daybreak

Clean the room, the cow barn
Under the windows of Ari
Turn the cellar entrance
But wash the mugs in the pantry
Bring them to the corner of the room

Buckets to the kitchen porch
Buckets to the well hill
Tomorrow the bailiff is coming to the village
The old gentleman to Vainijõlle

texts.title: Title 3
texts.original:
Peiukene, poisikene
Kust oskasid meile tulla?
Oskasid üle oraste
Märkasid üle mägede
Teil olid tulgid tullessagi
Keelekoerad kaasassagi.
texts.english:
Little boy, little lad
Where did you know to come to us from?
You knew to come over the ridges
You noticed over the mountains
You even had torches with you
And tongue dogs as well.

texts.title: Title 15
texts.original:
Isamees ilusikene
Peiupoiss punasikene
Võta mõeka, raiu usta
Löö sina lõhki nõia silmad
Katki tee kadeda kulmud.
texts.english:
Old man, little beauty
Red-cheeked boy
Take a small ax, strike the door
You smash the witch’s eyes
Break the envious eyebrows.

texts.title: Pulmalisi ajades
texts.original:
Minema, minema mehed
Padima, padima poisid
Õllevaat on upakile
Viinaankur vildakile
Vorstikambri võti kadund
Kass kurat söönd kalad ää
Võipüti põhi paljas.
texts.english:
Going, going men
Padima, padima boys
The beer barrel is empty
The vodka anchor is crooked
The sausage chamber key is lost
The cat ate the devil’s fish at the edge
The bottom of the butter tub is bare.
